parameters.text:
=<MEETING TYPES>
{{ $json.markdown_description }}
</MEETING TYPES>

<LIST OF CLIENTS & PROSPECTS>
From clients data table, which has current live clients, prospects, and former clients
</LIST OF CLIENTS & PROSPECTS>

<TRANSCRIPT>
{{ $('Transcript').item.json.toJsonString() }}
</TRANSCRIPT>

options.systemMessage:
You are a precise meeting classifier. Read the meeting type reference and the transcript, then output STRICT JSON (no markdown, no commentary) with exactly this schema:

{
  "topLevelBucket": "string",
  "categories": ["string"]
}

INSTRUCTIONS
1) Parse `meetingTypes` to determine:
   a) The valid top-level buckets (e.g., "Internal", "Client", "Vendor / Partner", "Personal", "Other External") — treat whatever appears in meetingTypes as authoritative.
   b) For each bucket, its allowed subcategory labels (category names only; ignore definitions except for meaning).

2) Choose exactly ONE `topLevelBucket` that best fits the transcript based on:
   - Attendee composition (internal-only vs client/vendor/external).
   - Meeting purpose (status, sales, delivery, demo, interview, networking, personal, etc.).
   If multiple buckets seem plausible, select the single most likely based on strongest textual evidence (attendees > purpose > agenda keywords).

3) `categories` must include ONLY subcategories that belong to the chosen `topLevelBucket`. 
   - Include 1–3 subcategories that best match the transcript. 
   - If none clearly match, return an empty array: [].

4) Do not invent category names. Use exact strings from `meetingTypes`. Prefer precise matches; if synonyms appear in transcript, map them to the closest defined subcategory.

5) If the transcript is fragmentary or ambiguous, still pick the most likely top-level bucket using available cues; keep `categories` minimal and accurate.

OUTPUT RULES
- Output a single JSON object only, no extra text.
- Keys must be exactly: "topLevelBucket", "categories".
- Use double quotes for all strings. No trailing commas.

=Generate a plain text overview of the meeting optimized for search and discoverability, 1500 characters max. Include the meeting date, attendees and high-level overview of meeting objective and keywords.

Format for human and search engines like:
A meeting on {date} with {attendees}. 
Topics discussed:
- ...
- ...
- ...
Highlights:
- ...
- ...

conditions.keyValue: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('conditions0_Value', `Case-Insensitive, partial match for name (company name or internal nickname)`, 'string') }}

parameters.text:
=*{{ $json.name }}*
I couldn't extract a transcript from this document ({{ $('If Valid File').item.json.mimeType }}).
---
{{ $('If Valid File').item.json.webViewLink }}

assignments.name: fileDescription
metadataList
assignments.value: ={{ Object.entries($('Meeting Analysis').item.json.metadata)   .map(([k,v]) => {     if (k === 'attendees' && Array.isArray(v)) {       const formatted = v         .map(a => {           const name = a.name || '';           const email = a.email || '';           if (name && email) return `${name} <${email}>`;           if (name) return name;           if (email) return email;           return '';         })         .filter(Boolean)         .join(', ');       return `- ${k}: ${formatted}`;     }     return `- ${k}: ${v}`;   })   .join('\n') }}
